component.description: 1.5-A 42-V Step-Down DC-DC Converter With Eco-mode Control

absolute_maximum_ratings.parameter: PH DC voltage at low temperature

electrical_characteristics.conditions: No voltage hysteresis, rising and falling
No voltage hysteresis, rising and falling, 25°C

electrical_characteristics.parameter: Error amplifier transconductance (gm) during slow start
electrical_characteristics.raw_name: Error amplifier transconductance (gm) during slow start
electrical_characteristics.conditions: -2 µA < ICOMP < 2 µA, VCOMP = 1 V, VVSENSE = 0.4 V

electrical_characteristics.parameter: RT/CLK falling-edge to PH rising-edge delay
electrical_characteristics.raw_name: RT/CLK falling-edge to PH rising-edge delay
electrical_characteristics.conditions: Measured at 500 kHz with RT resistor in series

electrical_characteristics.raw_name: PWRGD switching threshold as % of the nominal VSENSE
PWRGD switching threshold as % of the nominal VSENSE
PWRGD switching threshold as % of the nominal VSENSE
PWRGD switching threshold as % of the nominal VSENSE

pin_definitions.description: A bootstrap capacitor is required between BOOT and PH pins. If the voltage on this capacitor is below the minimum required by the device, the output is forced to switch off until the capacitor is refreshed.
Error-amplifier output and input to the output-switch current comparator. Connect frequency-compensation components to this pin.
Enable pin, internal pullup current source. Pull below 1.2 V to disable. Float to enable. Adjust the input undervoltage lockout with two resistors.
The GND pin is the ground pin.
The PH pin is the source of the internal high-side power MOSFET.
An open-drain output, asserts low if output voltage is low due to thermal shutdown, dropout, overvoltage, or EN shutdown.

Resistor timing and external clock. An internal amplifier holds this pin at a fixed voltage when using an external resistor to ground to set the switching frequency. Pulling the pin above the PLL upper threshold causes a mode change whereby the pin becomes a synchronization input. Disabling of the internal amplifier occurs, and the pin is a high-impedance clock input to the internal PLL. If clocking edges stop, re-enabling of the internal amplifier occurs, and the mode returns to a resistor-set function.

Slow-start and tracking. An external capacitor connected to this pin sets the output rise time. The voltage on this pin overrides the internal reference, allowing use of the pin for tracking and sequencing.
The VIN pin is the input supply voltage which is from 3.5 to 42 V.
The VSENSE pin is the inverting node of the transconductance (gm) error amplifier.
GND pin must have an electrical connection to the exposed pad on the printed-circuit board for proper operation.

logical_pins.name: BOOT
logical_pins.description: A bootstrap capacitor is required between BOOT and PH pins. If the voltage on this capacitor is below the minimum required by the device, the output is forced to switch off until the capacitor is refreshed.

logical_pins.name: VIN
logical_pins.description: The VIN pin is the input supply voltage which is from 3.5 to 42 V.

logical_pins.name: EN
logical_pins.description: Enable pin, internal pullup current source. Pull below 1.2 V to disable. Float to enable. Adjust the input undervoltage lockout with two resistors.

logical_pins.name: SS/TR
logical_pins.description: Slow-start and tracking. An external capacitor connected to this pin sets the output rise time. The voltage on this pin overrides the internal reference, allowing use of the pin for tracking and sequencing.

logical_pins.name: RT/CLK
logical_pins.description: Resistor timing and external clock. An internal amplifier holds this pin at a fixed voltage when using an external resistor to ground to set the switching frequency. Pulling the pin above the PLL upper threshold causes a mode change whereby the pin becomes a synchronization input.

logical_pins.name: PWRGD
logical_pins.description: An open-drain output, asserts low if output voltage is low due to thermal shutdown, dropout, overvoltage, or EN shutdown.

logical_pins.name: VSENSE
logical_pins.description: The VSENSE pin is the inverting node of the transconductance (gm) error amplifier.

logical_pins.description: Error-amplifier output and input to the output-switch current comparator. Connect frequency-compensation components to this pin.

logical_pins.name: GND
logical_pins.description: The GND pin is the ground pin.

logical_pins.name: PH
logical_pins.description: The PH pin is the source of the internal high-side power MOSFET.

logical_pins.name: Thermal pad
logical_pins.description: GND pin must have an electrical connection to the exposed pad on the printed-circuit board for proper operation.
